title: Silicon Motion Shows Off Enterprise SM8008 and SM8388 SSD Controllers at Computex 2026
gist: At Computex 2026 Silicon Motion demoed two enterprise PCIe Gen5 SSD controllers: the high-performance MonTitan SM8388 for nearline storage (up to 128TB, 3.5M read IOPS) and the low-power SM8008 boot-drive controller (<4W, integrated power loss protection). The piece flags that surging NAND prices are shrinking the controller's share of SSD BOM cost, changing vendor dynamics.

insights.claim: Silicon Motion's SM8388, part of its MonTitan platform, is an 8-channel PCIe Gen5 x4 enterprise controller announced in late 2025 and now shipping in silicon, aimed at nearline enterprise storage.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: A now-in-silicon Gen5 enterprise controller signals imminent design wins; watch for OEM SSD launches adopting MonTitan in coming months.
insights.quote: First up, we have the SM8388, which was announced in late 2025 and is now in silicon.

insights.claim: The SM8388's 21mm x 21mm package is small enough to fit not just U.2/U.3 and E.3 form factors but even the smallest E.1 drives, widening its addressable drive designs.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Broad form-factor compatibility, including compact E.1, expands the TAM for a single controller SKU across dense server storage designs.
insights.quote: the 21mm x 21mm controller being small enough to be used in not only U.2/U.3 form factor and E.3 form factor drives, but even the smallest E.1 drives as well
insights.locator: Part of Silicon Motion's MonTitan platform

insights.claim: The SM8388 supports both TLC and QLC NAND and capacities up to 128TB, letting SSD vendors tune the same controller for write performance or for maximum capacity.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: TLC/QLC flexibility and 128TB support position the controller for the capacity-driven nearline/AI-storage buildout; a single design serves both perf and capacity tiers.
insights.quote: the high-performance controller is meant to be used with both TLC and QLC NAND, giving SSD vendors the option to tune for write performance or capacity

insights.claim: The SM8388 is rated for sequential reads and writes up to 14.4GB/second (the effective ceiling of a PCIe Gen5 x4 link) and over 3 million random IOPS.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Hitting the Gen5 x4 bandwidth ceiling means the interface, not the controller, is the bottleneck, foreshadowing demand for PCIe Gen6 controllers next.
insights.quote: the controller is rated to deliver sequential read and writes up to 14.4GB/second, the effective limit of a PCIe Gen5 x4 connection, or over 3 million IOPS for random I/Os

insights.claim: In a live Computex demo the SM8388 reference drive sustained sequential writes over 9.3GB/sec and reads at 14.6GB/sec, with random performance of 450K sustained write IOPS and 3.5M read IOPS.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Live-demoed 3.5M read IOPS validates the spec sheet under high queue depth, de-risking the controller for storage OEM adoption decisions.
insights.quote: the demo showcased the drives hitting 450K IOPS for sustained writes, and a blazing 3.5M IOPS for reads
insights.locator: Meanwhile, in terms of random IOPS

insights.claim: Silicon Motion's SM8008 is an 8-channel PCIe Gen5 x4 boot-drive controller targeting M.2 2280 internal boot drives and E1.S external boot storage, distinct from the performance-focused SM8388.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: A dedicated boot-drive SKU shows Silicon Motion segmenting its enterprise lineup to capture the high-volume, lower-performance boot niche alongside high-perf nearline.
insights.quote: the company also had a PCIe Gen5 x4 boot drive controller on display as well, the SM8008

insights.claim: The SM8008 delivers over 14GB/second sequential reads and 2.3M random read IOPS, deliberately below the SM8388's performance tier.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Even the boot-focused part nears Gen5 bandwidth limits, showing how commoditized high sequential throughput has become across controller tiers.
insights.quote: over 14GB/second for sequential reads and 2.3M IOPS for random reads is nothing to sneeze at, either
insights.locator: In terms of performance, the 8-channel drive

insights.claim: The SM8008's marquee feature is low power combined with integrated power loss protection: the TSMC 6nm-fabbed controller targets SSDs drawing under 4W total, easing the load needed to enable power loss protection.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Sub-4W draw plus integrated PLP on TSMC 6nm targets data-center boot deployments where power/reliability trump raw speed; a differentiator versus higher-power controllers.
insights.quote: The TSMC 6nm-fabbed controller is designed to go into SSDs that will draw under 4W in total, which makes for a light load on the drive to help enable its power loss protection capabilities
insights.locator: Instead, the marquee feature of the boot drive controller

insights.claim: Dramatically rising NAND prices have shrunk the SSD controller's share of overall drive BOM cost, shifting the economics of the storage supply chain.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: As NAND dominates BOM, controller price competition matters less to drive cost, potentially improving controller-vendor margins and pricing leverage.
insights.quote: With NAND prices increasing so dramatically, SSD controllers have become a lower percentage of the overall BOM cost.

insights.claim: Because controllers are now a smaller BOM slice, SSD manufacturers use market pricing changes to evaluate and switch between different controller vendors.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Vendor evaluation driven by market pricing means controller share can shift; monitor which controllers win designs as a leading indicator of vendor momentum.
insights.quote: One impact is that SSD manufacturers use market pricing changes to evaluate different vendors.

insights.claim: Controllers demoed at Computex typically translate into shipping SSD designs within the following months, making trade-show appearances a leading signal of near-term product launches.
insights.speaker: Ryan Smith
insights.so_what: Use Computex controller reveals as an early read on the next few months' SSD launch pipeline and Silicon Motion design-win cadence.
insights.quote: Seeing these designs at Computex usually means we will see more SSD designs with them in the coming months.
